info.title: SmartlyQ API
info.description:
# SmartlyQ API

REST API for the SmartlyQ platform: generate content, manage social posts, chatbots, media, and more.

## Quick Start

1. Get an API key from [app.smartlyq.com/my/developer](https://app.smartlyq.com/my/developer).
2. Send your first request:

```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" https://api.smartlyq.com/v1/me
```

## Authentication

All requests (except health/docs) require a **Bearer token**. Use your API key as the token:

- **Key types:** `sqk_live_*` (live) or `sqk_test_*` (test/sandbox).
- **Header:** `Authorization: Bearer sqk_live_xxxx` or `Authorization: Bearer sqk_test_xxxx`.

| Scope | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `articles:read` | List and get articles |
| `articles:write` | Generate, delete articles |
| `images:read` | List and get images |
| `images:write` | Generate, delete images |
| `videos:read` | List and get videos |
| `videos:write` | Generate, delete videos |
| `presentations:write` | Generate presentations |
| `social:read` | List accounts and posts |
| `social:write` | Create, update, delete posts |
| `audio:read` | Get audio resources |
| `audio:write` | Text-to-speech, speech-to-text |
| `urls:read` | List and get short URLs and stats |
| `urls:write` | Shorten, delete URLs |
| `captain:use` | AI Captain messages and conversations |
| `chatbot:use` | Chatbots, train, messages, conversations |
| `media:read` | List and get media |
| `media:write` | Upload, delete media |
| `analytics:read` | Overview, posts, account analytics |
| `jobs:read` | List and get async jobs, cancel |
| `seo:read` | Keyword research, SERP, rank tracking, competitors, backlinks, on-page audits |

## Billing & Credits

- **Prepaid wallet:** API usage is deducted from your Developer API wallet (credits in SQC).
- **Pricing:** See the [pricing table](https://smartlyq.com/pricing) and in-app Developer dashboard for per-operation costs.
- **How it works:** Each billable request (e.g. generate, post, chatbot message) consumes credits; response may include `usage` with `cost` and `balance_remaining`.
- **Top up:** Add credits via [app.smartlyq.com/my/developer](https://app.smartlyq.com/my/developer) (wallet / top-up).

## Rate Limiting

Limits apply per API key (and may be enforced per user or IP). Default is 60 requests per minute per key (overridable per key).

Response headers:

| Header | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `X-RateLimit-Limit` | Max requests per window |
| `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | Remaining in current window |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | Unix timestamp when the window resets |

When exceeded: HTTP `429` with `Retry-After` header.

## Errors

Errors are JSON with a consistent shape:

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "ERROR_CODE",
    "message": "Human-readable message",
    "details": {}
  },
  "meta": { "request_id": "...", "timestamp": "..." }
}
```

| HTTP | Code | Meaning |
|-----|------|---------|
| 400 | `BAD_REQUEST` | Malformed or invalid request |
| 401 | `UNAUTHORIZED` | Missing or invalid API key |
| 402 | `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE` | Not enough credits |
| 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | Valid key but insufficient scope or access |
| 404 | `RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND` | Resource does not exist |
| 409 | `CONFLICT` | Conflict (e.g. duplicate idempotency) |
| 422 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Validation failed (fields in details) |
| 429 | `RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | Too many requests |
| 500 | `INTERNAL_ERROR` | Server error |

## Async Jobs & Polling

Some operations (e.g. article or video generation) return **HTTP 202 Accepted** with a `job_id`. Poll for status:

- **Poll:** `GET /jobs/{job_id}` until `status` is `completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`.
- Response includes `result` or `error` when finished.

## Webhooks

Configure webhook URLs in the Developer dashboard. We send HTTP POST to your URL with a signed payload.

- **Verification:** Validate signature using your webhook secret (see dashboard).
- **Events:** e.g. `job.completed`, `job.failed`, `chatbot.conversation.ended`.

## Idempotency

For POST/PUT/PATCH, send **`X-Idempotency-Key`** (unique value per logical operation). Within 24 hours, the same key returns the same response without re-executing.

## Pagination

- **Offset:** `page` (1-based) and `per_page` (default 20, max 100).
- Response includes `pagination` with `page`, `per_page`, `total`, `pages`.

## Versioning

Current version is **v1**. Base path: `https://api.smartlyq.com/v1`. Future versions will use new path prefixes (e.g. `/v2/`).

tags.name: SEO
tags.description: DataForSEO-backed keyword research, SERP, rank tracking, competitors, backlinks and on-page audits

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Subscribe to event notifications (HMAC-signed deliveries with retries).

tags.name: Shorts
tags.description: Magic Shorts: turn long videos into ranked viral clips

tags.name: Presentations
tags.description: AI presentation (slide deck) generation from a prompt

post.description: Start article generation. Returns 202 with job_id; poll GET /jobs/{job_id} for result. Either `topic` or `keywords` must be provided.
Returns 202 with job_id for async generation. Either `prompt` or `description` must be provided.

get.description:
Returns every model currently available for video generation, including valid parameter values for each model and generation type.

Call this endpoint before submitting a generation request to discover what options a specific model supports. Empty arrays (`[]`) mean the parameter is not applicable. Boolean `supports_*` flags indicate optional feature availability.

200.description: List of available models with their capabilities

post.description:
Submits a video generation request. Returns `202 Accepted` immediately with a `job_uid`; poll `GET /v1/jobs/{job_uid}` for status and the final video URL.

**Before submitting:** call `GET /v1/videos/models` to retrieve the valid parameter values (`duration`, `resolution`, `mode`, `aspect_ratio`, etc.) for your chosen model. Passing an unsupported value returns a `422` validation error.

**Billing:** the cost is deducted from your API wallet when the job is accepted. If video generation fails on the provider side the charge is automatically refunded.

value.prompt: A lone astronaut walks across a crimson desert at sunset
Camera slowly pulls back to reveal the full scene

get.description: Lists available AI avatars and talking photos for avatar video generation. Not billed.
Lists available voices (id, name, language, gender) for avatar videos. Not billed.

post.description:
Generates a talking-avatar video from a script. Returns `202 Accepted` with a `job_uid`; poll `GET /v1/avatar-videos/{uid}` for status and the final video URL.

**Before submitting:** call `GET /v1/avatar-videos/avatars` and `GET /v1/avatar-videos/voices` to pick a valid `avatar_id` + `voice_id`.

**Billing:** the cost is deducted from your API wallet when the job is accepted. If the render fails the charge is automatically refunded.
Re-voices an existing video into other languages. Returns `202 Accepted` with a `job_uid`; poll `GET /v1/avatar-videos/{uid}`. Billed per request from your API wallet; refunded on failure.

source_video_url.description: URL of the video to translate.

post.description: Drives an avatar with a provided audio track. Returns `202 Accepted` with a `job_uid`; poll `GET /v1/avatar-videos/{uid}`. Billed per request from your API wallet; refunded on failure.

audio_url.description: URL of the audio to lipsync.

post.summary: Generate a video from a prompt
post.description: Prompt-to-video. From a single prompt the agent writes the script, picks the avatar and voice, and renders - no `avatar_id`/`voice_id` needed. Returns `202 Accepted` with a `job_uid`; poll `GET /v1/avatar-videos/{uid}`. Billed per request from your API wallet; refunded on failure.

prompt.description: What the video should be about, e.g. 'A 30-second product launch announcement, upbeat and confident'.

get.description: Returns the status and (when complete) the video URL for an avatar-video render.
Returns all connected social media accounts for the authenticated user.

post.description: Keyword suggestions for a seed keyword with real monthly search volume, CPC and competition.

200.description: Keyword suggestions with volume, CPC and competition

post.description: Live Google organic SERP for a keyword: ranked results, SERP features and People Also Ask.

200.description: Organic SERP results plus SERP features

results.title: Best SEO Tools (2026)
results.description: Compare the top SEO tools for 2026.

people_also_ask: What is the best SEO tool?

post.description: Bulk keyword difficulty scores (0-100) for a list of keywords.
Every keyword a domain ranks for, with position, search volume and estimated traffic.

200.description: Ranked keywords with position, volume and ETV

post.description: Organic rank overview for a domain: estimated keyword count, traffic value and position buckets.
Domains overlapping a target on shared keywords, with common-keyword counts and traffic.
Backlink profile summary for a domain: authority rank, totals, referring domains, broken links and link types.
Instant on-page SEO audit for a single URL: on-page score and flagged checks.
Find link-building prospects: referring domains that link to competitors of the target but not to the target itself.
List the top referring domains for a domain by authority, with backlink counts, first-seen date and dofollow counts.
List the top backlink anchor texts for a domain, with backlink and referring-domain counts.
Get the backlink spam/toxicity score (0-100) for one or more domains.

Historical organic rank/traffic trend by month. Premium lookup. Async: returns 202 + job_uid - poll GET /jobs/{job_uid} for the result.

Historical organic keyword count and traffic (ETV) trend by month. Premium lookup (priced ~10x a normal SEO call).

202.description: Accepted - job queued. Poll GET /jobs/{job_uid}; on completion result_data contains the same payload the old 200 body carried.

post.description:
Deep crawl-based technical site audit. Async: returns 202 + job_uid - poll GET /jobs/{job_uid}; result_data carries the audit (typically 30-90s).

Crawl-based technical SEO audit: overall on-page score, crawl issues, and the worst-scoring pages with their flagged checks. Slower (~10-15s).

202.description: Accepted - job queued. Poll GET /jobs/{job_uid}; on completion result_data contains the same payload the old 200 body carried.

post.description:
AI Visibility brand/domain citation lookup. Async: returns 202 + job_uid - poll GET /jobs/{job_uid} for the result (may take ~1 minute).

How often ChatGPT and Google AI Overview cite a brand, domain or keyword: total mentions, AI search volume, estimated impressions (split by platform), the citing prompts, cited source domains, related pages and a 12-month trend.

value.description: Brand name, domain, or keyword to look up.

type.description: Whether `value` is a brand/keyword or a domain.

202.description: Accepted - job queued. Poll GET /jobs/{job_uid}; on completion result_data contains the same payload the old 200 body carried.

post.description:
Run a prompt across ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini/Perplexity. Async: returns 202 + job_uid - poll GET /jobs/{job_uid} for the result (1-2 minutes).

Run one prompt across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini and Perplexity side-by-side. Returns each model's answer text, citations and fan-out queries; pass `brand` to get a mentions_brand flag per answer.

prompt.description: The prompt to run across the AI models.

models.description: Which models to query (default all four).

web_search.description: Allow the models to use web search.

country.description: Two-letter ISO country code to geolocate web search (e.g. 'US').

brand.description: Optional brand to flag mentions of in each answer.

example.prompt: What is the best CRM for a small agency?

202.description: Accepted - job queued. Poll GET /jobs/{job_uid}; on completion result_data contains the same payload the old 200 body carried.

post.description: Zero-input GEO audit: give a website URL and the audit auto-generates realistic customer queries in the market's language (or accepts your own), runs every query across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini and Perplexity, and scores unaided brand presence. The result contains an AI Presence Score (0-100 = share of query x model checks whose answer mentions the brand or domain), per-query mention results, and the competitors AI recommends instead. Long-running (typically 5-15 minutes): returns 202 with a job_id - poll GET /jobs/{job_id}. Billed per (query x model) check.

url.description: The business website URL to audit.

brand.description: Brand name to detect in answers (auto-detected from the site when omitted).

location.description: Market/location name (queries are generated in its language).

language.description: ISO language code override for generated queries.

queries.description: Custom customer queries (max 12). Omit to auto-generate 8 from the website.

models.description: Which models to check (default all four).

202.description: Accepted; poll job for the audit report
Message accepted; AI Captain reply included

post.description: Triggers training using the chatbot's existing training data (training_text, training_url, and FAQs). No request body required. Poll GET /chatbots/{id}/train-status for completion.

202.description: Training accepted; poll train-status for completion

200.description: List of messages in the conversation

post.description: Cancels a queued or processing job. The request body may be empty, but `Content-Type: application/json` **must** still be set — omitting it returns a 400 error.

children.description: Nested replies, same shape as a comment.

remote_id.description: Platform-side id of the created reply.

meta_window_open.description: Meta only: whether the 24h reply window is open.

get.description: List the workspace's webhook subscriptions and the available event names. Requires scope `webhooks:read`.

post.description: Subscribe a public HTTPS URL to events. The signing `secret` is returned once at creation. Requires scope `webhooks:write`. Max 10 active webhooks per workspace. Events: post.published, post.failed, post.partial, account.connected, account.disconnected, account.token_expired, comment.received, message.received, job.completed, job.failed.

url.description: Public HTTPS endpoint that receives signed POSTs.

secret.description: Signing secret — returned ONLY here, at creation. Store it now.

delete.description: Delete a webhook subscription. Requires scope `webhooks:write`.

post.description: Begin connecting a NEW social account from your own UI (no SmartlyQ dashboard session needed). Returns a one-time `connect_url` (valid 15 minutes) — open it in a browser so the user can authorize. After they approve, the platform's OAuth callback completes the connection and (if provided) redirects to `return_url` with `?connected=<count>&platform=<name>`. Supported on all platforms. Platforms that expose multiple targets (YouTube channels, Google Business locations, Tumblr blogs, LinkedIn pages) connect them all automatically. Poll completion with `GET /social/connect/{state_token}`. Requires scope `social:write`.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Platform to connect. All 13 platforms are supported (use `twitter` for X, `gmb` for Google Business).

return_url.description: Optional URL to redirect the user to after they finish authorizing.

connect_url.description: Open in a browser to authorize. Expires in 15 minutes.

get.description: Poll whether a headless connection finished, using the `state_token` embedded in the `connect_url` returned by `POST /social/connect/{platform}`. Useful when your app can't observe the `return_url` redirect (server-to-server, or the user closed the tab). Verifies the signed token and reports `connected` (with the new account), `pending`, or `expired`. Requires scope `social:read`.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: For this GET, pass the `state_token` from the `connect_url` (NOT a platform name).

post.description: Writes ONE scroll-stopping hook line (<=8 words) to overlay on a short's first seconds. Provide `script` or `transcript_words`. Billed per request from your API wallet; refunded on failure.
Suggests B-roll moments (with timings) for a word-level transcript. Billed per request; refunded on failure.
Picks on-screen emphasis moments (punchy full-screen text with timings) from a word-level transcript. Billed per request; refunded on failure.
Generates a viral, click-optimized thumbnail from a video title (optionally guided by a reference image) and returns a hosted `image_url`. Billed per request; refunded on failure.

image_url.description: Optional https reference image whose subject is preserved.

post.description: Writes ONE platform-tuned social caption (with hashtags) for a short-form video. Billed per request; refunded on failure.

content.description: What the video says (transcript) so the caption is on-topic.

post.summary: Generate viral shorts from a long video
post.description: Turns one long video into ranked, ready-to-post vertical (9:16) clips with hooks + virality scores. Provide `video_url` (direct media) OR `source_url` (a YouTube/Vimeo/TikTok link). Returns `202 Accepted` with a `job_uid`; poll `GET /v1/shorts/{uid}`. Billed per REQUESTED clip from your API wallet; a wholly-failed job is refunded automatically.

video_url.description: Direct media URL (provide this or source_url).

clip_count.description: Clips to produce (3-20). Billed per requested clip.

202.description: Accepted — poll GET /v1/shorts/{uid} for the ranked clips

get.description: Lists your workspace's Magic Shorts jobs.
Gets a Magic Shorts job's status and its ranked clips (hooks, scores, clip URLs). Poll after `generateShorts` until `status` is `completed`.

200.description: The shorts job and its clips.

post.description:
Generates a complete AI presentation (slide deck) from a single prompt. Returns `202 Accepted` immediately with a `job_id`; poll `GET /v1/jobs/{job_uid}` for status. Generation typically takes 60-120 seconds. When the job completes, its `result` contains the `presentation_id` and a `url` that opens the finished deck in the presentations editor.

**Billing:** a flat charge per generated deck is deducted from your API wallet when the job is accepted. If generation fails the charge is automatically refunded.

Requires the `presentations:write` scope.

prompt.description: The topic or brief for the deck - what the presentation should cover, who it is for, and any angle to take.

language.description: BCP-47 language tag for the deck copy (e.g. en-US, de-DE, el-GR).

tone.description: Overall tone for the deck copy, e.g. professional, casual, persuasive.

example.prompt: A product launch deck for an AI scheduling assistant, aimed at small marketing agencies

202.description: Accepted - poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_uid} for the finished deck

BearerAuth.description: API key from Developer dashboard (Bearer token).

VideoIdParam.name: video_id
VideoIdParam.description: The unique video ID (hex string, e.g. 8a3621ff57d6587c4dbb68f6d803f6cc69d8a42b6ae84)

Conflict.description: Conflict (e.g. duplicate idempotency or already-finished job)

keywords.description: Keywords to base the article on

model.description: Model identifier. See GET /v1/videos/models for available values.

type.description: Generation mode. `text_to_video` requires `prompt`. `image_to_video` requires `image_url`; `prompt` is optional motion guidance.

prompt.description: Text description of the video. Required for text_to_video. Optional for image_to_video. Max length varies per model — see prompt_max_length in /v1/videos/models.
prompt.example: A lone astronaut walks across a crimson desert at sunset

image_url.description: Source image URL for image_to_video. Must be a publicly accessible JPEG or PNG.

duration.description: Video length in seconds. Valid values vary per model — see duration in /v1/videos/models. Defaults to the shortest valid duration.

resolution.description: Output resolution (e.g. 720p, 1080p). Valid values vary per model — see resolution in /v1/videos/models.

mode.description: Generation quality mode (e.g. Standard, Professional, Fast). Valid values vary per model — see mode in /v1/videos/models.

aspect_ratio.description: Output aspect ratio (e.g. 16:9, 9:16, 1:1). Valid values vary per model — see aspect_ratio in /v1/videos/models.

style.description: Visual style preset (e.g. Anime, Cyberpunk). Only supported by some models — see style in /v1/videos/models.

movement.description: Camera/subject movement amplitude (Auto, Small, Medium, Large). Only supported by some models — see movement in /v1/videos/models.

negative_prompt.description: Elements to suppress in the output. Only supported by some models — see supports_negative_prompt in /v1/videos/models.

seed.description: Reproducibility seed. Only supported by some models — see supports_seed in /v1/videos/models.

generate_audio.description: Generate ambient audio alongside the video. Only supported by some models — see supports_generate_audio in /v1/videos/models.

camera_fixed.description: Lock the camera position (no camera movement). Only supported by some models — see supports_camera_fixed in /v1/videos/models.

duration.description: Valid duration values in seconds. Empty array means duration is not configurable.

resolution.description: Valid resolution values. Empty array means resolution is not configurable.

mode.description: Valid mode values. Empty array means mode is not applicable.

aspect_ratio.description: Valid aspect ratio values. Empty array means aspect ratio is not configurable.

style.description: Valid style preset values. Empty array means style is not supported.

movement.description: Valid movement values. Empty array means movement is not supported.

prompt_max_length.description: Maximum prompt length in characters. 0 means no enforced limit.

job_uid.description: Use with GET /v1/jobs/{job_uid} to poll for status.

url.description: CDN URL of the generated video (available once completed).

account_ids.description: IDs of connected social accounts to post to

media_urls.description: Media URLs to attach to the post

link.description: URL to include with the post

media_urls.description: Media URLs to attach to the post

link.description: URL to include with the post

SocialPostUpdateRequest.description: At least one field must be provided.

voice.description: Voice to use for synthesis. Defaults to alloy if omitted or invalid.

audio_url.description: Publicly accessible direct URL to the audio file (mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, wav, or webm). Must return raw audio bytes — Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, and iCloud share links will not work as they return HTML instead of the file. The URL must be reachable without authentication.

message.description: User message to the AI Captain

filename.description: Name of the file to upload

upload_url.description: Presigned URL to PUT your file to

media_id.description: ID of the created media record
